shortDescription.text: Session Fixation via missing session regeneration on login
Logout endpoint accessible via GET request enables CSRF-triggered logout

message.text: The login handler assigns req.session.userId without calling req.session.regenerate() first. An attacker who sets a known session ID (via cookie injection or URL fixation) before the victim logs in can hijack the authenticated session, since the session ID persists unchanged across the authentication boundary. The code comments at lines 104-115 explicitly acknowledge this vulnerability and show the fix (wrapping in req.session.regenerate()) but it is not applied.

The logout endpoint is mapped as GET /logout (index.js line 41) and the handler destroys the session without any CSRF token verification. An attacker can craft an tag or link pointing to /logout to force-logout a victim, enabling denial-of-service or session fixation attack chains (force logout then fixate a new session). Logout should use POST with CSRF protection.
